headline: 27% of Americans Own ETFs but Allocate Just 6%, Here’s Why That Costs Them Retirement Income
summary: The Charles Schwab Modern Wealth Survey 2025 found that 27% of American investors own ETFs, yet those funds account for only 6% of the average portfolio. Individual stocks: 25%; mutual funds: 13%; cryptocurrency: 10%. The mismatch matters most for pre-retirees, because dividend-focused ETFs package the income-producing equity asset class into a single low-cost holding that ... 27% of Americans Own ETFs but Allocate Just 6%, Here’s Why That Costs Them Retirement Income

headline: PepsiCo vs Coca-Cola: A Decade-Long Fight for Dominance
summary: PepsiCo (NASDAQ: PEP) and Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO) just delivered first quarter results that expose a widening gap between the two beverage giants. Coke leaned on Zero Sugar momentum and a near pure-play concentrate model. Pepsi leaned on snacks, international resilience, and an affordability reset to nudge North American volumes back to life. Zero Sugar Lifts ... PepsiCo vs Coca-Cola: A Decade-Long Fight for Dominance

headline: Coca-Cola Consolidated to invest $35m in US facility
summary: The US Coca-Cola bottler said it plans to add a new production line at the Indiana site, “specifically for bottling beverages in glass bottles”.

headline: 1 Consumer Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Avoid
summary: Consumer staples stocks are solid insurance policies in frothy markets ripe for corrections. But they’re also double-edged swords as they often lag in booming conditions, and this pattern has persisted recently. Over the past six months, the industry’s 2.6% return has trailed the S&P 500 by 3.9 percentage points.

raw.headline: 1 Consumer Stock for Long-Term Investors and 2 We Avoid

Over the past six months, the industry’s 2.6% return has trailed the S&P 500 by 3.9 percentage points.

headline: 'The stock market is not the economy,' says Rebecca Homkes
summary: Strategist Rebecca Homkes weighs in on the broader impacts of the Iran War, warning against trying to correlate U.S. equity indices with the economy and exploring the real impact of AI on the labor market.
